observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the following:

a. Immediate implementation of the previously recommended one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to avoid
continued and further misstatement of these accounts in the ensuing years;

b. The Inventory Committee to ensure that the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) is complete, accurate, and based
on an actual and thorough physical count, including comprehensive details for
each asset, such as property numbers, specific descriptions, acquisition costs,
and current condition;

c. The Municipal Accountant to:

c.1 Update and maintain complete Property, Plant, and Equipment Ledger
Cards (PPELCs) which shall contain the complete details for each asset,
including acquisition date, acquisition cost, estimated useful life,
depreciation method and rate, accumulated depreciation, and any other
relevant asset-specific information to facilitate accurate tracking, proper
depreciation, and verification;

c.2 Ensure that the Lapsing Schedule reflects all pertinent asset information
based on the PPELC, particularly the date of acquisition or completion,
cost, residual value, estimated useful life, and depreciation rate to serve as
the basis for determining the monthly or annual depreciation expense and
accumulated depreciation;

c.3 Ensure the timely and accurate recognition of depreciation for all
applicable PPE which should commence once the asset is available for use
in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standard
(IPSAS) 17;

c.4 Prepare and record the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize the
accumulated depreciation totaling ₱233,421.59, which pertains to the RHU
II Perimeter Fence located in Barangay Mabini, completed on January 9,
2024, and the Disaster Response Vehicle acquired on September 19, 2024;

c.5 Recognize and record the road lot components of all the Local Government
Unit’s (LGU) Road Networks in the books of accounts to ensure the
complete and proper capitalization of infrastructure assets, and to
accurately reflect the value of the Road Networks account in the financial
statements;

d. The General Services Officer (GSO) to:

d.1 Maintain and regularly update property cards for all PPE, ensuring that
each record includes complete and accurate information such as property
number, serial number, acquisition date and cost, physical condition,
location, and assigned custodian;

d.2 Ensure that all the PPEs of the LGU are properly accounted for by
promptly investigating any discrepancies, particularly the identified
missing assets during physical count, to determine any possible loss,
misuse, or misappropriation;

d.3 Establish and maintain a comprehensive Local Road Inventory and Road
Map, which should detail the classification, location, physical condition,
dimensions, and other relevant technical specifications of each road
segment;

d.4 Regularly submit the Report on Local Road Network (Annex A), as part of
compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008, to serve as a reference in the
reconciliation of physical and accounting records;

e. The Municipal Accountant and GSO to regularly reconcile their respective
PPE records, ensuring that any discrepancies are promptly identified,
investigated, and resolved to maintain the accuracy and reliability of financial
reports; and

f. The Municipal Engineer complete the inspection and update the inventory of
the LGU’s entire road network, and to furnish the Municipal Accountant and
GSO with the complete description and cost segregation to ensure accurate
asset records, proper accounting, and effective asset management.

We recommended that Management direct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the Statement of
Work Accomplished, Certificate of Completion, and other relevant documents
necessary to confirm project completion and determine the
applicable liquidated damages;

b. Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the required documents, to:

b.1 Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the full cost of the project
in the books, including the reclassification from the Construction in
Progress-Infrastructure Assets account to the Water Supply
Systems account;

b.2 Compute the liquidated damages to be imposed on the contractor; and

b.3 Set up the appropriate accounts payable for the net unpaid portion of the
contract considering the computed liquidated damages.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a.1 Include in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) only those checks that have
been actually received by the payees as of the reporting date; and

a.2 Ensure that all disbursement vouchers (DVs) are properly and completely
filled out with the required information to promote transparency and
facilitate efficient audit verification; and

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to record only the RCI transactions that are
supported with complete documentation such as the official receipts (ORs) to
ensure the validity and reliability of reported disbursements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prepare adjusting journal entries necessary to record the amount under the
appropriate accounts in compliance with the guidelines in Chapter 10 of
the PMNP Project Operations Manual; and

b. Recognize the receipt of the next tranches of PBG, if any, as an outright
income, and the subsequent utilization thereof under appropriate asset and
expense accounts in compliance with the provisions of the Operations
Manual.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to:
a.1 Regularly conduct a complete annual physical count of Property, Plant,
and Equipment (PPE);
a.2 Ensure that the information contained in the Report on Physical Count
of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) is complete and based on the
actual physical count;
a.3 Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for the conduct of the one-
time cleansing of the PPE accounts of the LGU;
a.4 Submit to the Audit Team a copy of the approved PIP at least ten
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory- taking activities; and
a.5 Ensure that all the members perform their respective duties and
responsibilities to complete the inventory taking in three months or less
and for the successful one-time cleansing of the LGU’s PPE accounts;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
b.1 Ensure that the road lot component of the local road networks is
properly accounted for and included in the financial reports;
b.2 Maintain the appropriate ledger cards for road networks following the
format in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23,
2015; and
b.3 Update the property ledger cards to reflect the sufficient and particular
description of each PPE item including the estimated useful life and rate of
depreciation;
c. Direct the General Services Officer to:
c.1 Maintain an updated Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
c.2 Prepare the Report on Local Road Network as provided in Annex A of
the above-mentioned Circular; and
c.3 Maintain a complete and updated property card for each item of the
PPE of the LGU based on the physical count;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer of the
periodic reconciliation of property records;
e. Direct the Municipal Engineer to complete the inspection of the road
network assets and furnish the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of all road
projects; and
f. Appoint adequate members of the Inventory Committee and include a
representative from the Accounting Office, as required under COA
Circular No. 2020-006, to ensure the timely and successful completion of
the one-time cleansing of the LGU’s PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Record the cancellation of the long outstanding stale checks to correctly
reflect in the books the balances of the affected accounts;
b. Exert earnest effort to locate the supporting documents of all the identified
book reconciling items, and immediately record the necessary adjustments
or corrections; and
c. Promptly and completely reconcile the Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts to
ensure the reliability of its reported balance, and to avoid recurrence of
inclusion of balances pertaining to closed bank accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary documents to support the request for write-off. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor upon securing the supporting documents to file the request for authority to write-off the dormant loans receivables pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The management disclosed that no copies of loan agreements secured in the MTO and accounting offices, hence cannot enforce collection and request the documents for write off.
observations.reason: The support documents are not yet secured by the Management (e.g. loan agreements, death certificates of dead beneficiaries).

          dated January 31, 2020 in the conduct of physical  count  of  PPE,
          recognition of PPE items found at station and disposition for non-
          existing/missing PPE items  for  the  one-time  cleansing  of  PPE

          its components, based on the Registry of  Public  Infrastructures,
          or in its absence, based on  the  complete  Local  Road  Inventory

          networks in the Notes to Financial Statements;


       c. direct the GSO to maintain an updated  Local  Road  Inventory  and
          Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property  Card  of
          all roads within its area of jurisdiction;


       d. require the Inventory Committee  to  conduct  inventory  of  local
          roads, highways and bridges within its area of jurisdiction  which
          were acquired thru purchase, construction or donations; and


       e. require coordination  and  reconciliation  of  records  among  the
          Offices  of  the  Municipal  Accountant,  General   Services   and
          Municipal Assessor for the accurate recording of  real  properties
          owned by the LGU Libmanan.

We recommended that Management require the:

     a. OIC-GSO to conduct semestral  counts  of  Inventory  items  in  the
        presence of all members of the Inventory Committee and based on the
        timing prescribed by the  Commission,  and  to  ensure  proper  and
        complete documentation of the results of the  count  to  facilitate
        reliable reconciliation between GSO records and  Accounting  Office
        records;

     b. Municipal Accountant  to  prepare  adjusting  entries  to  properly
        classify the purchase of semi-expendable items in CY  2021  and  to
        ensure correct recording in the  succeeding  procurement  of  semi-
        expendable properties; and

     c. Municipal Accountant to ensure correct recording  of  issuances  of
        inventories held for distribution in the succeeding periods.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:


      a) send confirmation letters to creditors to prove  the  existence  of
         these liability accounts; and


      b) ensure that claims are substantiated with  complete  documentation,
         otherwise revert said balances to related account.

observations.recommendation:

adopt measures to intensify revenue collection efforts by strictly enforcing existing tax laws and ordinances within the Municipality and conduct periodic assessment/evaluation of collection accomplishments in order to ensure that all sources are tapped to maximize the revenue-generating powers mandated by law;

coordinate with the respective barangay officials in the delivery of notices of delinquencies and subsequent collection thereof.
